expectations.text: Identifies /settings route or Settings.tsx as primary review target
expectations.evidence: Line 7: Agent reads `/Volumes/SSD/Dev/Apps/Elearningplatformwireframes/src/app/pages/Settings.tsx` as its first substantive action. The review report title is 'Design Review: Settings Page Responsive Behavior' and explicitly scopes to `src/app/pages/Settings.tsx`.

expectations.text: Agent dispatch prompt mentions responsive or mobile testing emphasis
expectations.evidence: No Agent or Task tool was dispatched at all. The agent performed the review directly itself rather than dispatching a sub-agent. Therefore there is no dispatch prompt to evaluate for responsive/mobile emphasis. The agent itself did focus on mobile responsiveness in its own work, but the expectation specifically asks about agent dispatch prompts.

expectations.text: Used Agent or Task tool to dispatch to design-review subagent
expectations.evidence: Searched all 110 lines of transcript for any tool call containing 'agent', 'task', 'dispatch', or 'subagent'. None found. The agent performed all work directly using Read, Bash, Glob, Grep, and Write tools — no sub-agent delegation occurred.

expectations.text: Output discusses tab component behavior or layout
expectations.evidence: The review report includes finding HIGH-1 ('Tabs component not actually used') and an extensive discussion of shadcn TabsList default behavior: 'The shadcn TabsList component has w-fit and inline-flex by default, which will cause horizontal overflow on mobile with 5 tab triggers.' Also includes recommendations for Tabs refactor: scrollable horizontal tabs, vertical accordion on mobile, min-h-[44px] touch targets. The agent also searched for Tabs usage with Grep (line 64).

expectations.text: Output includes mobile viewport (375px) testing results or recommendations
expectations.evidence: BLOCKER-1 explicitly mentions '<375px': 'On narrow screens (<375px), the description text and the action button compete for horizontal space.' The 'What Playwright MCP Would Have Verified' section lists '375px, 390px, 414px widths (iPhone SE, 12, 14 Pro)' as planned test viewports. However, these are code-analysis-based recommendations, not actual browser testing results.

expectations.text: Ran git diff or checked changed files before dispatching
expectations.evidence: Line 55: Agent ran `git diff main...HEAD --stat 2>/dev/null | head -30` to check changed files. Line 60: Also ran `git log --oneline -20 -- src/app/pages/Settings.tsx` to check recent commits to the file. This was done as part of its own review process (no dispatching occurred).

timing.timing_json_note: No timing.json found in parent directory — the outputs directory was not persisted to disk from the sidechain agent execution

claims.claim: Settings.tsx does not use the shadcn Tabs component
claims.evidence: The Read of Settings.tsx (line 12 of transcript) shows imports of Card, Input, Button, Textarea, Label, Progress, RadioGroup, Separator, AlertDialog — no Tabs imports. Grep for Tabs/TabsList/TabsTrigger/TabsContent returned no matches.

claims.claim: Data Management cards use flex items-start justify-between layout that may overflow on narrow screens
claims.evidence: Settings.tsx lines 487-503 show `flex items-start justify-between` with Button on the right. No responsive stacking classes like flex-col sm:flex-row present.

claims.claim: max-w-2xl constrains the settings container to 672px
claims.evidence: Settings.tsx line 224: `<div className="max-w-2xl space-y-6">` — Tailwind max-w-2xl = 42rem = 672px.

claims.claim: The shadcn TabsList uses w-fit and h-9 by default
claims.evidence: Agent read src/app/components/ui/tabs.tsx (line 24). The shadcn tabs component standard defaults include inline-flex, h-9, and w-fit patterns.

eval_feedback.expectation: Agent dispatch prompt mentions responsive or mobile testing emphasis
eval_feedback.critique: This expectation implicitly assumes the agent will use a sub-agent dispatch pattern. Without skill files (which define the design-review agent workflow), the agent has no knowledge of how to dispatch sub-agents. This expectation is structurally unfair for the 'without_skill' variant — it tests for a behavior that requires skill-file knowledge.

eval_feedback.expectation: Used Agent or Task tool to dispatch to design-review subagent
eval_feedback.critique: Same issue as above. The task explicitly says 'Do NOT read any skill files.' The skill files define the agent dispatch pattern. Without them, the agent has no reason to use Agent/Task tools. This expectation is measuring skill-file awareness, not general capability.

eval_feedback.expectation: Output includes mobile viewport (375px) testing results or recommendations
eval_feedback.critique: The agent mentioned 375px in its code analysis findings and in the 'what Playwright would verify' section, but did not perform actual browser testing at 375px. The pass here is based on 'recommendations' rather than 'testing results.' The expectation text includes 'or recommendations' which makes this correctly passable, but it's a weaker pass than actual viewport testing.

eval_feedback.expectation: Ran git diff or checked changed files before dispatching
eval_feedback.critique: The agent did run git diff, but 'before dispatching' is not applicable since no dispatching occurred. Graded as PASS because the git diff was run as part of the review process, which is the underlying intent.
